comments.body: The current JSON RFC requires UTF-8. Would you rather a 400 or 406 over ignoring the charset? (I'm not a Spring user so I can't respond in that context.) Why would you want to not use UTF-8 anyway?
@TomBlodget once you use any non-english character, UTF-8 becomes obstruction. UTF-16 might be better in that regard, but there are specific situations which just can't be handled by UTF.
Unfortunately, your solution does not seem to work. I've added tests that check the endpoint for different encodings (Commit 86dc434351d7ee2c142afc8f389e7837810b19f6)
I wanna get my bounty xD

@JonathanJohx: I also think that Jackson is component that is in charge of handling the encoding. However, to the best of my knowledge, Jackson only supports Unicode encodings (UTF-8, UTF-16 etc.). In order the have it read other encodings, you need to override much more code and initialize it with a Reader instance so it doesn't do anything with the encoding anymore.
If you add the reference for this statement By default charset is UTF-8, i will upvote.

answers.body:
By default charset is UTF-8, MappingJackson2HttpMessageConverter is who manages the charSet. You can override by implementing the bean and setting charSet to null.

@Bean
public MappingJackson2HttpMessageConverter mappingJackson2HttpMessageConverter() {
    MappingJackson2HttpMessageConverter jsonConverter = new MappingJackson2HttpMessageConverter();
    ObjectMapper objectMapper = new ObjectMapper();
    objectMapper.setSerializationInclusion(JsonInclude.Include.NON_NULL);
    jsonConverter.setObjectMapper(objectMapper);
    jsonConverter.setDefaultCharset(null);
    return jsonConverter;
}

I created a pull request with these changes on your repository pullRequestCharacterEncoding and works well. Kind regards.

Please try the following in your request:

Accept-Charset: <US-ASCII or any charset you want to set>

Please check the below link for more details:

https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Accept-Charset

Please let me know if it works for you

title: How to handle character encoding in spring RestController
body:
I'm trying to find some information on how I should handle character encoding in my RestController.

Spring seems to completely ignore the charset sent in the Content-Type header. No matter which charset is sent to the server using this header, the server tries to read it as UTF-8 and sends response with charset UTF-8.

Here is a GitHub repository with a demo reproducing this scenario: https://github.com/hawk1234/characterEncodingTest

Can someone point me where is it described in spring documentation? I came across Content Negotiation but I think it is more about response format rather then encoding.

UPDATE 2018-11-01

I've implemented some tests to better show what is the problem. Commit 86dc434351d7ee2c142afc8f389e7837810b19f6 includes class EncodingTest which tests endpoint using different encodings.

I've also implemented my encoding handling (Commit a26c5da0b25fba8a40e3be41e967e6b1e5c4d546) however it is very basic implementation and may not take all scenarios into account. I'm still looking for easier solution using existing libraries.
